build_from.reason: This is the closest genuinely `.atcr/`-independent, cross-run, lazily-created append-only JSONL store in the codebase (os.UserConfigDir()-rooted, not `.planning/`-rooted), exactly as the epic's own Refinements section (item 2, confirmed) already identified. Its Append()/ReadRecords()/ReadAll() functions demonstrate the concurrency-safe pattern (single os.OpenFile O_APPEND write per record, one os.Write() call per record — never batched through a shared bufio.Writer — to preserve POSIX atomic-append guarantees) and the malformed/forward-incompatible-schema skip-and-warn read path this epic's new `.atcr/`-scoped TD store should copy.
build_from.suggested_approach: Do not import internal/scorecard directly (it is deliberately global/per-user, not per-repo). Instead create a new package (recommended: internal/localdebt; name TBD in design) that copies the Append/ReadAll/lazy-mkdir/month-or-run-shard pattern from internal/scorecard/store.go, rooted at `.atcr/debt/` (or similar) instead of `os.UserConfigDir()`. Reuse internal/history's Record-shape lesson (stable content-hash ID via FindingID in internal/history/record.go:48) for the TD item's dedup key, since reconciled findings already carry file+line+problem needed to derive one.

existing_patterns.name: Append-only cross-run JSONL store (internal/scorecard)
existing_patterns.description: internal/scorecard/store.go:89 Append lazily creates its directory (0700), marshals one record to JSON, and issues exactly one os.OpenFile(O_APPEND)+Write per record so concurrent writers never tear a line (POSIX atomic-append guarantee for regular files). ReadRecords/ReadAll stream-parse line-by-line via bufio.Reader (not Scanner, to survive an over-long line), skip malformed lines and forward-incompatible schema_version records with a warning rather than aborting. Store lives at os.UserConfigDir()/atcr/scorecard/YYYY-MM.jsonl (global, per-user) — the ONE structural difference from what this epic needs (per-repo `.atcr/`). internal/scorecard/paths.go:23 DefaultDir resolves the user-config path and monthFromRunID derives the shard name.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The new local TD store's Append/read implementation — copy the atomic-append-per-record and malformed-line-skip patterns verbatim; change only the root directory resolution.

existing_patterns.name: Append-only per-repo JSONL ledger (internal/history) — CAUTION, now .planning/-scoped
existing_patterns.description: internal/history was ORIGINALLY a `.atcr/findings-history.jsonl` append-only ledger (internal/history/paths.go:LegacyLedgerPath still returns this path) but Epic 19.4 deliberately moved primary writes to `.planning/history/YYYY-MM.jsonl` (internal/history/paths.go:ShardDir) specifically because the private pipeline wanted version-controlled, sharded history. The legacy `.atcr/` path is now read-only fallback for pre-19.4 data. This is the inverse of what Epic 20.1 needs: a *primary*, not legacy-fallback, `.atcr/`-scoped store with zero `.planning/` dependency for standalone/public users.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Do not extend or re-route internal/history — it is intentionally private-pipeline-oriented post-19.4. Its Record struct (stable content-hash ID via FindingID, small intentional field set) and Append() (single-batch-buffer-then-one-Write, MkdirAll parent dirs) are still a good API shape to mirror in the new package, just not the package to reuse directly.

existing_patterns.name: Sharded technical-debt store + CLI (internal/debt, internal/tdmigrate, cmd/atcr/debt*.go)
existing_patterns.description: `atcr debt` already exists as a live Cobra command family (list/add/dashboard) reading the Epic-12.1 sharded store at `.planning/technical-debt/items/*.yaml` + the authoritative `.planning/technical-debt/README.md` markdown table, via internal/tdmigrate.ParseREADME/Item and internal/debt's insertRow/aggregate helpers. It is entirely `.planning/`-scoped (private pipeline only) and already registered as a routed command in skill/SKILL.md line 79's command table. docs/technical-debt.md documents the existing commands.
existing_patterns.follow_for: CLI shape precedent for a public equivalent: `atcr debt` already has list/add/dashboard subcommands. The public store this epic adds will likely need its own subcommand (`atcr debt resolve`) or a `--local`/no-`.planning/` mode rather than a brand-new top-level command, to avoid a second, confusingly-named `atcr` command family. skill/SKILL.md line 79 already documents `atcr debt` in its routing table — extending its subcommand surface is additive; the top-level `debt` row itself does not need to change.

existing_patterns.name: justification/back-reference already live on reconciled findings (Epic 18.2, referenced by the epic as 18.3)
existing_patterns.description: internal/reconcile/emit.go:154 JSONFinding already carries Justification (json:"justification,omitempty") and SourceReport (back-reference to the review.md narrative section), stamped by internal/reconcile/justification.go:72 stampJustifications during the normal `atcr reconcile` gate path (internal/reconcile/gate.go:255). This is an ATCR-internal extension of the public reconcile/finding.go library type, not part of the library's core Finding struct.
existing_patterns.follow_for: AC3's dependency (consuming justification/back-reference fields) is already satisfied in main — no need to wait on or re-verify a separate Epic 18.3 landing; the fields exist today in `reconciled/findings.json`.

related_files.purpose: The single dispatcher skill (Epic 20.0) that routes `/atcr <command>` to a live `atcr` CLI invocation. Already lists `atcr debt` in its command table (line 79) and documents the Prerequisites section (binary-on-PATH check, git-worktree check) this epic's CONVENTIONS.md extraction must pull out. The `debt` row is the extension point for `/atcr debt resolve`; subcommands are discovered via `atcr debt --help` per the documented convention.

Sibling on-demand skill file loaded by SKILL.md; shows the pattern for a secondary skill file loaded on demand, relevant if `/atcr debt resolve` needs its own on-demand instructions file the way host-review.md is separate from SKILL.md.

Go embed harness exposing SKILL.md and secondary skill files as build-time constants. Any new secondary skill file (e.g. skill/debt-resolve/SKILL.md) must be embedded here and any new on-demand pointer in SKILL.md must resolve to a sibling file.

Go test harness validating skill/SKILL.md's structure, including the dispatcherCommands list (skill/skill_test.go:133) that mirrors newRootCmd. `debt` is already in the list; a new top-level command would require an addition, but `debt resolve` as a subcommand of the existing `debt` command likely does NOT change this list. New tests may be needed to assert the debt-resolve subcommand is documented.

`atcr reconcile` command implementation — the natural integration point for AC2 (persist reconciled findings into the local TD store across runs), since it already has a `--no-scorecard` flag pattern (cmd/atcr/reconcile.go:43) that a `--no-local-debt` or similar flag could mirror. The hook would run after reconcile.RunReconcile returns (cmd/atcr/reconcile.go:90-99) and after the scorecard emit block (cmd/atcr/reconcile.go:110-111).

Where stampJustifications is invoked during the reconcile gate path (internal/reconcile/gate.go:255) — shows exactly where in the reconcile pipeline a new local-store persistence hook would need to run (after justifications are stamped, so the persisted record can carry them).
Documents the Epic 12.1 sharded-storage format history and its lessons (merge-conflict-prone single files vs. per-run shards) that the epic's Proposed Solution #1 explicitly says to learn from.

User-facing documentation for the scorecard store's format, CLI usage, privacy model, and schema-versioning conventions — the closest existing doc template for what this epic's AC1 ("local TD store format is defined and documented") and AC4 (docs/skill-usage.md update) should mirror in style and structure.
Existing public skill installation/usage guide that AC4 requires extending with the new `/atcr debt resolve` capability.

User-facing documentation for the existing `.planning/`-scoped `atcr debt` command family. Useful reference for the public debt-resolve docs and for explaining the distinction between private-pipeline debt and the new `.atcr/`-scoped local store.

Public library Reconcile entry point (reconcile/reconcile.go:80). The CLI cmd/atcr/reconcile.go calls internal/reconcile.RunReconcile; any persistence hook that also needs to work for library consumers should be considered here, though the epic's primary target is the CLI skill route.

Documents how private claude-prompts skills map to the `/atcr` dispatcher. May need a note if the debt-resolve skill is also expected to be migrated eventually, but this is out of scope for the initial implementation.

integration_points.description: Where a new local-TD-store-append call would be added, mirroring how scorecard emission is already wired into the reconcile command (via internal/scorecard, with a --no-scorecard opt-out flag). The natural placement is after the scorecard.EmitForReconcile call (~line 111) so the store receives the same reconciled Result that scorecard does.

The `/atcr debt resolve` route this epic adds is a subcommand of the existing `atcr debt` family, not a new top-level dispatcher row. SKILL.md already documents that top-level commands with subcommands (`debt`, `personas`, `models`, `benchmark`) expose subcommands via `atcr <command> --help`. The skill should either document `atcr debt resolve` explicitly in the debt row's description or add a dedicated subsection, without inventing subcommand names.

The point in the reconcile gate pipeline where justification/back-reference data is already attached to findings — a local-store persistence call should run after this so persisted TD records carry the enriched fields. In practice the CLI hook in cmd/atcr/reconcile.go receives the Result after stampJustifications has run, so no change to gate.go is required unless the hook is moved deeper into the engine.

New on-demand skill file (skill/debt-resolve/SKILL.md) implementing `/atcr debt resolve`. It reads the `.atcr/`-scoped local TD store, selects items (likely by severity/age or explicit user filter), and drives a RED→GREEN→ADVERSARIAL→REFACTOR resolution loop adapted to a repo-agnostic, `.planning/`-free context. Must reference skill/CONVENTIONS.md for binary-on-PATH and git-worktree checks rather than duplicating Prerequisites text.

The existing `atcr debt` command family registers list/add/dashboard subcommands. A `resolve` subcommand can be added here if the public resolve flow is exposed as `atcr debt resolve` (consistent with the `/atcr debt resolve` skill route), or it can live as a separate top-level command if design decides otherwise. The default path is to extend this family.

reusable_components.name: scorecard.Append / scorecard.ReadRecords / scorecard.ReadAll pattern
reusable_components.usage_example: Not directly importable (scorecard is user-config-global, not repo-local) — copy the atomic-append-per-record + malformed-line-skip + schema-version-skip pattern into a new `.atcr/`-scoped package.

reusable_components.name: scorecard.DefaultDir / monthFromRunID path resolution
reusable_components.usage_example: The new local store will need its own path resolver (repo-root/.atcr/debt/ instead of os.UserConfigDir()/atcr/scorecard) and its own shard naming. Follow the run_id-based month rotation or choose per-run shards; document the choice explicitly.

reusable_components.name: history.FindingID stable content-hash ID
reusable_components.usage_example: FindingID(file, line, problem) — sha256 over NUL-separated fields, first 8 bytes hex — mirrors internal/debate.itemID. The new TD store's dedup/identity key can reuse this exact construction so IDs are consistent with the rest of the codebase's finding-identity conventions.

reusable_components.name: atcr debt CLI subcommand family
reusable_components.usage_example: newDebtCmd() registers list/add/dashboard subcommands off `atcr debt`; a public equivalent can add a `resolve` subcommand to the same parent command if the new local store is exposed as `atcr debt <verb>` rather than a wholly separate command family.

reusable_components.name: stampJustifications narrative extractor
reusable_components.usage_example: Already stamps Justification and SourceReport onto JSONFinding records during reconcile. The debt-resolve skill consumes these fields directly from reconciled/findings.json (or from the local store records derived from them); no extension needed.

architecture_notes: internal/history's `.atcr/findings-history.jsonl` was a per-repo `.atcr/`-scoped append-only ledger until Epic 19.4 deliberately moved primary writes to `.planning/history/` for version-control-friendly sharding — meaning a `.atcr/`-scoped design was tried before and then intentionally superseded for the PRIVATE pipeline. This epic's `.atcr/`-scoped design is for a different audience (standalone/public users with zero `.planning/`), so the same superseding logic does not automatically apply, but the design should explicitly note why it is choosing `.atcr/` over `.planning/` (audience: no `.planning/` exists for these users) to avoid the appearance of contradicting Epic 19.4's decision.

Concurrent-write safety is a recurring, explicitly-documented concern across both existing stores (scorecard.go's long comment on O_APPEND atomicity, internal/history/writer.go's caveat about os.File.Write looping on short writes for large buffers). The new store's design must state its concurrency guarantee (likely: one Append call = one os.Write, no batching across concurrent `atcr reconcile` invocations) rather than leaving it implicit, and should reference the accepted TD-004 won't-fix stance.

`atcr debt` (private, .planning/-scoped) already exists as a live, tested CLI surface with list/add/dashboard subcommands — this epic is not inventing the concept of a debt CLI from scratch, only a `.atcr/`-scoped, `.planning/`-free sibling/extension of it. The naming and UX should avoid collisions: `atcr debt resolve` is the natural public subcommand, but it must not be confused with the `.planning/`-scoped `atcr debt list/add/dashboard`.

Epic 18.2's justification/SourceReport stamping is already live on main (internal/reconcile/emit.go, internal/reconcile/justification.go) — AC3's stated dependency on Epic 18.3 is effectively already satisfied; no blocking wait is needed before this epic's execution.

The local TD store becomes atcr's 6th append-only ledger (joining audit, debate, scorecard, tools, history). The project has an accepted, documented won't-fix on cross-process O_APPEND locking (TD-004) — the new store should state the same tradeoff explicitly rather than silently diverging.

The skill/CONVENTIONS.md extraction (Epic 20.0 addendum) is a deliverable of this epic. It should hold shared boilerplate currently embedded in skill/SKILL.md's Prerequisites section (binary-on-PATH check, git-worktree check, .atcr/ path-safety rules) so that skill/SKILL.md and skill/debt-resolve/SKILL.md can both reference it instead of duplicating text.

files_to_modify.reason: Document the `/atcr debt resolve` subcommand (extend the `atcr debt` row description or add a subsection) and, per Epic 20.0's addendum, extract shared Prerequisites boilerplate out to a new skill/CONVENTIONS.md
Hook local TD store persistence into the reconcile command so AC2 (persistence across runs) is satisfied, with a `--no-local-debt` opt-out flag mirroring `--no-scorecard`
Document the new `/atcr debt resolve` capability per AC4
Embed the new skill/debt-resolve/SKILL.md and skill/CONVENTIONS.md files as build-time constants

Add assertions that `/atcr debt resolve` is documented in SKILL.md and that CONVENTIONS.md is referenced. The dispatcherCommands list likely does NOT need a new entry because `debt` is already present and `resolve` is a subcommand.

Optionally register a `resolve` subcommand under `atcr debt` if the public resolve flow is implemented as a CLI subcommand rather than only a skill route. Design decision TBD; include as a likely-modification.

files_to_create.purpose: The .atcr/-scoped, append-only local TD store (package name recommended as internal/localdebt; final name TBD during design). Pattern informed by internal/scorecard/store.go.
Record type and stable FindingID helper for local TD store entries, informed by internal/history/record.go and internal/reconcile/emit.go JSONFinding.

New public skill implementing `/atcr debt resolve` — adapts /resolve-td's RED->GREEN->ADVERSARIAL->REFACTOR cycle to a repo-agnostic, .planning/-free context. References skill/CONVENTIONS.md for shared prerequisites.

Shared boilerplate extracted from skill/SKILL.md's Prerequisites section (binary-on-PATH check, git-worktree check, .atcr/ path-safety rules) per Epic 20.0's addendum, referenced by both skill/SKILL.md and skill/debt-resolve/SKILL.md

integration_gaps.description: How is the repo root detected for the `.atcr/` path? cmd/atcr/reconcile.go uses CWD as repo root (Root: "."). The new package should follow the same convention or use a shared helper (e.g. git rev-parse --show-toplevel) so the store is always scoped to the current repo.

Should the local store shard by month like scorecard/history, by run_id, or keep a single `.atcr/debt.jsonl`? The epic mentions learning from Epic 12.1 (single files vs. per-run shards). A decision and schema_version field are needed.

Does the hook append every reconciled finding, only findings at/above a severity threshold, or only findings not already present (dedup by FindingID)? The skill route's autonomy depends on having actionable, non-noisy items.

Will `/atcr debt resolve` read directly from `.atcr/debt/*.jsonl` or via a new `atcr debt resolve` CLI command? The former couples the skill to the file format; the latter keeps SKILL.md's CLI-invocation-only rule but requires a new CLI subcommand.

The `--no-local-debt` flag mirrors `--no-scorecard`, but should `atcr reconcile` also skip already-persisted findings (by FindingID) on re-reconcile of the same review dir? The store is append-only, so duplicates may accumulate unless dedup is handled at write time or read time.

skill/skill.go currently embeds SKILL.md, host-review.md, ambiguity-adjudication.md, and findings-format.md. CONVENTIONS.md and debt-resolve/SKILL.md must be added to the embed set, and skill_test.go should verify they are non-empty and referenced from SKILL.md.

test_patterns.test_location: co-located _test.go files per package (e.g. internal/localdebt/store_test.go, mirroring internal/scorecard/store_test.go)
